articles.title: DEEPSEEK POTENTIAL IPO
articles.summary: DeepSeek reportedly explored raising $1.5 billion at a $71 billion valuation before a possible IPO in late 2026 or 2027.

articles.title: KALSHI RAMPS UP EFFORT TO BUILD MARKETS FOR AI COMPUTING POWER
articles.summary: Kalshi has created a tool that plots the future price of computing power so users can develop a sense of where the price to rent GPUs is headed. It will use prediction markets to build the forward curve. Forward curves are used to plot expected future interest rates and moves by central banks. Compute is becoming a commodity in its own right as demand grows. Kalshi's forward curve is based on weekly and monthly event contracts related to compute costs, and it goes as far as a year into the future.

articles.title: GOOGLE ANNOUNCES GEMMA 4 OPTIMIZED FOR THE PIXEL 10'S TPU
articles.summary: Gemma 4 E2B for TPU is designed to run natively on the Pixel 10's TPU. It supports instant, deep offline conversation, image identification, and on-device audio transcription. The model also lets users command core phone functions, like Wi-Fi or maps, using just private voice or text. Video demonstrations of what the model can do are available in the article.

articles.title: A FRAMEWORK FOR FRONTIER AI AND THE DAWNING OF A NEW AGE
articles.summary: AGI, mimicking human cognitive capabilities, is likely just years away, predicts Demis Hassabis. This advancement marks a pivotal moment in human history, signaling a shift towards frontier AI capabilities.

articles.title: THE GUIDE TO SOFTWARE FACTORIES
articles.summary: Warp CEO Zach Lloyd argues cloud software factories will replace interactive coding agents by automating the entire software development lifecycle through orchestrated AI workflows. He outlines a factory architecture built around cloud runtimes, multi-model orchestration, human oversight, and continuous evaluation to improve engineering velocity while reducing cost, governance risks, and operational complexity.

articles.title: COMPILING AGENT SKILLS CUT TOKEN USE BY 94%
articles.summary: A recurring content workflow was converted from natural-language agent instructions into deterministic code, reducing token usage by 94%. The approach keeps language models focused on judgment-heavy steps while conventional software handles stable, repetitive operations.

articles.title: LAUNCH YOUR WEBSITE FASTER THAN EVER WITH FRAMER. NOW WITH AGENTS TO BRING SPEED AND FLEXIBILITY DIRECTLY TO YOUR WORKFLOW.
articles.summary: Framer is a professional website builder where AI agents make real, reviewable changes directly to your site. Trusted by teams at Miro and Perplexity, agents work across your canvas, components, CMS, and SEO to produce editable, production ready updates. Review every change in branches, compare diffs, and launch what works best.

articles.title: 👉 Launch your site with Framer today
WANDR BENCHMARK: EVALUATING RESEARCH AGENTS THAT MUST SEARCH WIDE AND DEEP
articles.summary: WANDR (Wide ANd Deep Research) is an open benchmark and evaluation harness built around realistic and challenging data-collection tasks for knowledge work. Wide and deep research allows agents to sustain broad discovery without sacrificing factual quality from one record to the next. WANDR captures that structure with a flexible, composable qualification key hierarchy.

articles.title: 5 TRENDS THAT DEFINED AI ENGINEERING AT WORLD'S FAIR 2026
articles.summary: AI engineering has come a long way in three years. The latest AI Engineer World's Fair shows just how much the field has matured. Engineering practices, such as building coding agents, designing harnesses, managing context, evaluating model outputs, and orchestrating increasingly autonomous systems, are now becoming part of mainstream software development. This post looks at the five largest trends that emerged during the AI Engineer World's Fair to see where AI engineering stands in 2026.

articles.title: ANNOUNCING BONSAI 27B: THE FIRST 27B-CLASS MODEL TO RUN ON A PHONE
articles.summary: PrismML announced Bonsai 27B, the first 27B-class model running on a phone, achieved through 1-bit and ternary weights, shrinking the model size to 3.9 GB and 5.9 GB respectively. Bonsai 27B supports complex tasks like multi-step reasoning and tool use while maintaining high performance, fitting securely within the device limits of modern smartphones.

articles.title: THE STATE OF OPEN SOURCE AI
articles.summary: Open weights are now where the work happens. A majority of production tokens now route through them. The five highest-volume models on OpenRouter are all open. Closed models still lead at the frontier, but the frontier is not what most workloads need.

articles.title: THE GREAT FLATTENING
articles.summary: Frontier coding models have shifted engineering's bottleneck from writing code to encoding judgment into agent harnesses that orchestrate planning, testing, review, and deployment. It is predicted that software organizations will flatten as cloud-based multi-agent systems replace traditional engineering workflows, leaving customer insight and product judgment as the primary human advantage.

articles.title: ELON MUSK QUIETLY BUYS A $1 BILLION GAS TURBINE COMPANY TO POWER GROK
articles.summary: Elon Musk has acquired APR Energy, a Jacksonville-based company that operates a fleet of mobile and diesel turbines generating more than 1 GW of capacity.

articles.title: MEASURING THE BUSINESS VALUE OF AI AGENTS
articles.summary: OpenAI outlined five practices for managing enterprise AI investments, emphasizing useful work per dollar rather than token prices alone.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC'S NEWEST AD IS CREEPING PEOPLE OUT
articles.summary: Anthropic's new ad, "There's hope in hard questions," unsettled viewers due to its dark imagery and critical AI commentary.

articles.title: CELEBRATING 25 YEARS OF VISUAL SEARCH INNOVATION
